characterCraft.note: At 160x360 the scarfed bird has a distinct helmeted silhouette, face, beak, articulated two-part wings, legs, scarf follow-through, and readable dive, soar, brace, impact, and run poses. It is at least as authored and legible as the protagonists in both references.

environmentCraft.note: Each frame reads as a built place without relying on the HUD: layered skies, weather, distant landmarks, midground silhouettes, terrain materials, vegetation, inhabited towers, and foreground props meet the reference-world density.

levelVariety.note: Five named biomes pair different landmark compositions with nine materially different hill silhouettes and five tower structures. The native sheet shows stronger environment-to-environment distinction than a palette swap and meets both reference progressions.

animationImpact.note: Aligned character crops prove pose motion, while the tower sequence clearly moves from forecast to strike to a spatially broad physical collapse and settled aftermath. The cleaned apex keeps one focal payoff line and meets both references for impact staging.

readability.note: The bird, gold flight rings, selected core, terrain edge, momentum bar, and tower silhouette stay separable at native size. Impact-label coalescing and reticle suppression remove the earlier payoff clutter, meeting both references.

artDirectionCohesion.note: The soft pixel storybook palette, repeated material highlights, geometric landmark language, gold reward grammar, and scarfed skybird remain coherent across all five visually distinct biomes and match the deliberate authorship of both references.
